assertedBy: OpenEvo Computational Curriculum Studies Working Group
rationale: BIO-CORE's Inheritance (OE-CONCEPT-000112) is scoped narrowly and exclusively to "transmission of genetic information from parent to offspring" — a single-system, biology-only definition consistent with BIO-CORE's decentralized, gene-centered framing throughout. OE- INTERDISCIPLINARY's Inheritance (OE-CONCEPT-000201) treats the same biological case as one of three co-equal disciplinary instances (biology, culture, technology) of a single general process, and is declared skos:broader of nothing else in its own vocabulary while itself being skos:narrower of Evolutionary Process (OE-CONCEPT-000090). The biology-discipline sense of OE-INTERDISCIPLINARY's Inheritance is arguably extensionally identical to BIO-CORE's Inheritance, which would favor skos:exactMatch — but this record is deliberately conservative (skos:closeMatch, status: contested) because BIO-CORE's Inheritance carries no explicit disciplinary scoping (it has no sibling "culture" or "technology" definition to be co-equal with), so whether the two concepts are truly interchangeable or merely overlapping is left open pending Phase 2 domain-editor review, consistent with the pluralism requirement (Spec §8.6) that this disagreement be representable as data rather than resolved by fiat here.
subject: BIO-CORE-v1.0.0:OE-CONCEPT-000112
